tripinfo.note: Mirrors scenarios/66-tripinfo-arrivallane's tripinfo tolerance (single absolute tolerance applied to every numeric tripinfo field by TripInfoComparator.Compare; arrivalLane is EXACT string match). 0.05 comfortably covers golden.tripinfo.xml's own 2-3 decimal rounding while still catching a real behavioral divergence. The scenario's own regression test additionally asserts the rerouting vehicle's engine.CompletedTrips RouteLength against the golden tripinfo routeLength with a 1.0m tolerance (see RungHDgap2RerouteRouteLengthTests.cs) -- looser than this comparator's 0.05 to allow for the possibility (not observed on this scenario: both engines run the identical RerouteEdgeWeights congestion model against the stopped 'blocker' vehicle and pick the same reroute instant/edges) that engine vs vanilla SUMO pick a different reroute instant.
